perturbation.original_text: Royalty: In consideration of the rights granted to Licensee pursuant to this Amendment 2, Licensee shall pay to Fox, or such other party as Fox may designate in writing, a royalty in the following amount: (a) From the first unit sold, Fox shall earn a royalty at the rate of ***** percent (*****%) of Licensee's Gross Receipts (as defined in the Agreement) from Licensee's sale and distribution of the IA2 Audio and Video Wireless Products. ("IA2 Audio and Video Royalty").
perturbation.changed_text: Royalty: In consideration of the rights granted to Licensee pursuant to this Amendment 2, Licensee may pay to Fox, or such other party as Fox may designate in writing, a royalty in an amount to be determined at Fox's sole discretion. The rate of royalty can be renegotiated depending on market conditions. (a) From the first unit sold, Fox shall earn a royalty at a rate determined by Fox. ("IA2 Audio and Video Royalty").
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that Licensee 'shall pay' a royalty at a specific percentage. The modified text changes 'shall pay' to 'may pay' and removes the fixed percentage, stating that the royalty amount is 'to be determined at Fox's sole discretion.' The rate of royalty can be renegotiated depending on market conditions. This contradicts the establishment of a fixed royalty rate.
perturbation.location: B. IA 2 AUDIO AND VIDEO WIRELESS PRODUCTS ROYALTY: 1.
perturbation.contradicted_location: B. IA 2 AUDIO AND VIDEO WIRELESS PRODUCTS ROYALTY: 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: IA 2 AUDIO AND VIDEO WIRELESS PRODUCTS ROYALTY: 1. Royalty: In consideration of the rights granted to Licensee pursuant to this Amendment 2, Licensee may pay to Fox, or such other party as Fox may designate in writing, a royalty in an amount to be determined at Fox's sole discretion. The rate of royalty can be renegotiated depending on market conditions. (a) From the first unit sold, Fox shall earn a royalty at a rate determined by Fox. ("IA2 Audio and Video Royalty"). For the avoidance of doubt, Licensee may not recoup any portion of the Ice Age 2 Guarantee (as defined in Amendment 1) through its sale and distribution of the IA2 Audio and Video Wireless Products.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the Licensee 'shall pay' a royalty at a defined percentage. The modified text changes this to 'may pay,' removes the fixed percentage, and makes the royalty amount discretionary to Fox, allowing renegotiation based on market conditions. This introduces a strong contradiction because the original agreement mandates a specific royalty, while the revised version makes the royalty payment optional and the amount variable, contradicting the expectation of a fixed and obligatory royalty payment as outlined previously. The contradicted text is also the same as the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: All VGSL and other CSP payments remitted directly to Fox pursuant to the VGSL Agreement and other CSP agreements with respect to the IA Audio and Video Wireless Products shall be treated in the same manner as set forth in Paragraphs 7(b)(ii) and 7(b)(iii) of the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: All VGSL and other CSP payments remitted directly to Fox pursuant to the VGSL Agreement and other CSP agreements with respect to the IA Audio and Video Wireless Products can be treated in a different manner. Fox has the sole discretion, it can change the way the payments should be treated based on market conditions or financial circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that VGSL and other CSP payments 'shall be treated in the same manner' as specified in Paragraphs 7(b)(ii) and 7(b)(iii) of the Agreement, indicating a fixed procedure. The modified text replaces 'shall be treated' with 'can be treated in a different manner' and states that 'Fox has the sole discretion' to change the payment treatment based on unspecified conditions. This introduces a contradiction by allowing for deviation from the previously established payment procedure.
perturbation.location: B. IA 2 AUDIO AND VIDEO WIRELESS PRODUCTS ROYALTY: 2.
perturbation.contradicted_location: B. IA 2 AUDIO AND VIDEO WIRELESS PRODUCTS ROYALTY: 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: VGSL and other CSP Accounting: All VGSL and other CSP payments remitted directly to Fox pursuant to the VGSL Agreement and other CSP agreements with respect to the IA Audio and Video Wireless Products can be treated in a different manner. Fox has the sole discretion, it can change the way the payments should be treated based on market conditions or financial circumstances. ***** The omitted portions of this exhibit have been filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission pursuant to a request for confidential treatment under Rule 406 promulgated under the Securities Act of 1933. Source: GLU MOBILE INC, S-1/A, 3/19/2007 3. No Direct-to-Consumer Distribution Royalty: Licensee shall not be entitled to receive a share of any revenues and shall have no claim to any revenues earned and collected by Fox, or a third-party Fox designates, for the IA2 Audio and Video Wireless Products which Fox distributes through its and its assigns' direct-to-consumer distribution channels.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates a fixed payment procedure as set forth in Paragraphs 7(b)(ii) and 7(b)(iii), but the changed text grants Fox the discretion to deviate from this procedure based on unspecified conditions, creating a direct contradiction regarding how payments related to IA Audio and Video Wireless Products should be handled. This contradicts the initial fixed procedure, making enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: Licensee shall not be entitled to receive a share of any revenues and shall have no claim to any revenues earned and collected by Fox, or a third-party Fox designates, for the IA2 Audio and Video Wireless Products which Fox distributes through its and its assigns' direct-to-consumer distribution channels.
perturbation.changed_text: Licensee may be entitled to receive a share of revenues earned and collected by Fox, or a third-party Fox designates, for the IA2 Audio and Video Wireless Products which Fox distributes through its and its assigns' direct-to-consumer distribution channels, based on a separate agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states that the 'Licensee shall not be entitled' to any revenues from Fox's direct-to-consumer distribution. The modified text replaces 'shall not be entitled' with 'may be entitled' and introduces the possibility of revenue sharing 'based on a separate agreement'. This creates a direct contradiction, making it unclear whether the licensee is excluded from these revenues or could potentially receive a share based on an unspecified agreement. It introduces uncertainty and ambiguity to the revenue sharing terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Direct-to-Consumer Distribution Royalty: Licensee may be entitled to receive a share of revenues earned and collected by Fox, or a third-party Fox designates, for the IA2 Audio and Video Wireless Products which Fox distributes through its and its assigns' direct-to-consumer distribution channels, based on a separate agreement. Except as herein expressly amended or by necessary implication modified by this Amendment, the Agreement in all other respects is hereby ratified and shall continue in full force and effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly denies the licensee any revenue share, whereas the changed text introduces the possibility of revenue sharing based on a separate agreement. The contradicted text repeats the language of the changed text, confirming that the changed text now conflicts with the original intention of the agreement. This directly contradicts the original stance of no revenue share, creating an in-text contradiction as it is unclear whether revenue sharing is permitted or not for direct-to-consumer distribution. The structural flaw, placement of conflicting statements within the contract, directly impacts the enforceability and interpretation of the revenue terms.
